figures.caption: Table 1: Empirical size at the nominal 5% level. Each entry is based on 1,000 replications and uses the original nominal calibration of the corresponding method.

Figure 1: Empirical power under IID errors. The rows correspond to the finite-rank, fast-decay, and slow-decay innovation spectra, and the columns correspond to ssig = 5 and ssig = 20 randomly selected signal directions. Each point is based on 100 paired replications.

Table 2: Single-change localization accuracy at SNR = 0.1 with (n, p) = (200, 21) and τn = 100. Entries are MAE (RMSE), measured in observation indices. Each entry is based on 100 paired replications.

Figure 2: Empirical power under FAR errors with ∥Ψ∥op = 0.5. The panel arrangement is the same as in Figure 1. BGHK uses setting-specific size-corrected critical values, whereas the other methods retain their original calibrations. Each point is based on 100 paired replications.
Table 3: Multiple-change localization measured by the true-to-estimated distance in (5.1). Entries are mean (standard deviation) over 100 paired replications.
Table 4: Multiple-change localization measured by the estimated-to-true distance in (5.2). Entries are mean (standard deviation) over 100 paired replications.

Figure 3: Sydney annual minimum-temperature curves around the RHT change point. The left panel shows the phase mean curves before and after the retained WBS change. The right panel shows their daywise difference and its 21-term Fourier representation.

Table 5: Whole-sample p-values and sliding-window rejection frequencies for the Sydney annual minimumtemperature curves. The complete sample contains n = 167 curves and uses p = 21 Fourier coefficients. Window entries are percentages.
Figure 4: Fourier decomposition of the Sydney phase means. The upper panel shows the coefficients of the two fitted phase means. The lower panel shows the signed coefficients of their difference.

Figure 5: NOAA ERSSTv6 Niño 1+2 analysis. The left panel shows the annual monthly curves and the two fitted segment means. The middle panel is the RHT localization path selected by the smallest marginal ridge p-value. The right panel shows the sample standard deviations of the retained Fourier scores.
